# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '*'
jobs:
  publish-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: 3.8
          
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install poetry
          poetry install --no-dev
        
      - name: Build application
        run: make build  # replace "make build" with the appropriate command for your project
      
      - name: Publish to PyPI
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags')
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
        with:
          user__pypi_token: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: 14
          
      - name: Install npm dependencies
        run: npm ci
        
      - name: Build NPM Package
        run: npm run build  # replace "npm run build" with the appropriate command for your project
      
      - name: Publish to npm
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags')
        uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v1
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Create Release
        id: create_release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # Needed to tag the release and publish it
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
          body: Auto-generated release for tagged commit ${{ github.sha }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
